storyInfo: He has returned to the mortal realm in the form of a stone. Much has changed in His long years of slumber, but the conflicts of man still remain.

attributes.content: You still won't leave?
attributes.name: Ju

attributes.content: That one started this war which has ravaged the lives of thousands. We were not the ones who began it. Why is it we who have to leave?
attributes.name: Bewildered Mountain Dweller

attributes.content: It' is intentionally causing strife, and it is my duty to intervene as fellow kindred. But this is not your fight.
attributes.name: Ju

attributes.content: Long have we followed your teachings. If you choose to stay, so shall we.
attributes.name: Bewildered Mountain Dweller

attributes.content: ...Is this your doing too, Carpenter?
attributes.name: Ju

attributes.content: The old man's hair is white, and his eyes have begun to grow cloudy.
But there is fire in his stubborn brow, the strength of which has not faded in the slightest.
You should not have involved them in this mess. How could you be so stubborn?
attributes.name: Ju

attributes.content: You knew—
attributes.name: Ju

attributes.content: Are you not just as stubborn? Your method of 'ending conflict' is not something that can be forced.
attributes.name: Old Carpenter

attributes.content: You know as well as I do that throwing yourself into a conflict is not the way to resolve it.
attributes.name: Old Carpenter

attributes.content: If you dislike conflict, you should not intervene or chase us away, and you most certainly should not... make such futile attempts to end conflict.
attributes.name: Old Carpenter

attributes.content: The wise stay away from danger... but it is not the solution I am looking for.
attributes.name: Ju

attributes.content: (Sigh)
attributes.name: Old Carpenter

attributes.content: If you will not leave, we can seek another solution. The land is vast, and there must be another way!
attributes.name: Old Carpenter

attributes.content: If we find the legendary Stone... the divine destiny of Yan.
attributes.name: Old Carpenter

attributes.content: This Stone is hardly easy to find...
attributes.name: Ju

attributes.text: He personally dispersed them, and eventually closed His in the hunt, but the stone was never found.
Yet He was not only searching for the stone, the solution.
He wanted there to be no conflict, no suffering across the land.

attributes.content: You mean this stone will make all your dreams come true?
attributes.name: Old Wood

attributes.content: There is a stone in the mountains, inside which dwells a deity.' That's what the books say, but who can truly tell if they're real or not?
attributes.name: Returned Peddler

attributes.content: If I get my hands on the stone, I'll dream about endless fertile fields!
attributes.name: Old Wood

attributes.content: Why not dream about endless granaries filled with grain?
attributes.name: Old Man from Neighboring Town

attributes.content: I've got two arms and two legs, I ought to plant my own food. Granaries filled with grain? I doubt the deity in the stone would be willing to see someone who wants to reap without sowing.
attributes.name: Old Wood

attributes.content: What are you trying to say?
attributes.name: Old Man from Neighboring Town

attributes.content: What? What did you think I was trying to say?
attributes.name: Old Wood

attributes.content: At it again, you two? Come on—
attributes.name: Returned Peddler

attributes.content: Stop it!
attributes.name: Indistinct Voice

attributes.content: Demon stone! Demon Stone!
attributes.name: Immature Voice

attributes.content: STOP!
attributes.name: Indistinct Voice

attributes.content: What are you?!
attributes.name: Little Wood

attributes.content: The stone leaves the hand with a strange cry.
The girl picks up the stone again, then cranks her shoulder, tightening her body like a bow.
NOT INTO THE RIVER!
attributes.name: Indistinct Voice

attributes.content: Wh-What are you?!
attributes.name: Little Wood

attributes.content: ...You can hear me?
attributes.name: Ju

attributes.content: Of course I can!
attributes.name: Little Wood

attributes.content: Ju looks at Himself. The elaborate clockwork of his body is no more; all that is left is this broken, fist-sized core.
Centuries ago, His followers had salvaged it from the Great Hunt, preserving a vestige of His existence.
Interesting... I have been awake for a few years and seen many people on this river bank. You are the first to hear my voice.
attributes.name: Ju

attributes.content: As the girl readies to throw the stone again—
Stop it, you brat!
attributes.name: Ju

attributes.content: I was a... craftsman. I was imprisoned in this stone because of the mistakes I made.
attributes.name: Ju

attributes.content: 'Mistakes I made?' I knew I should have thrown you!
attributes.name: Little Wood
Ju
attributes.content: It's not what you think—

attributes.name: Ju
attributes.content: No! Stop!
Didn't your parents teach you how to behave?
attributes.name: Ju

attributes.content: It's none of your business!
attributes.name: Little Wood

attributes.content: I don't take many students... but you'll study with me from this day forth! You ought to learn some rules!
attributes.name: Ju

attributes.content: I'd rather throw you into the water!
attributes.name: Little Wood

attributes.content: Little Wood—
attributes.name: Gentle Voice

attributes.content: Chief!
attributes.name: Little Wood

attributes.content: Why are you playing by the river again? I told you, the river's sick. You should stay away.
attributes.name: Town Chief

attributes.content: The girl hurriedly puts her stone away and walks towards the town chief, her cheeks puffed red.
Your dad's talking to folks from nearby towns about how to heal the river. I'm going there now... you should go home. This place is dangerous.
attributes.name: Town Chief

attributes.content: I'm coming with you!
attributes.name: Little Wood

attributes.content: ...Very well. But behave yourself.
attributes.name: Town Chief

attributes.content: You're finally here! These two are at it again.
attributes.name: Returned Peddler

attributes.content: Hmph.
attributes.name: Old Wood & Old Man from Neighboring Town

attributes.content: How old are you guys? You act like children!
attributes.name: Little Wood

attributes.content: Go sit in a corner, girl! We're talking about serious business.
attributes.name: Old Man from Neighboring Town

attributes.content: I heard everything you were talking about! Some stone? Does this look like one that makes dreams come true?
attributes.name: Little Wood

attributes.content: (Girl!)
attributes.name: Ju

attributes.content: ...Let's get back on track. Basically, the river is yellow and salty from this town to the next one down the river. It can't even be used for irrigation, much less drinking.
attributes.name: Town Chief

attributes.content: Do we know the reason?
attributes.name: Returned Peddler

attributes.content: I went up the north mountain to check on the source, but there doesn't seem to be anything wrong there.
attributes.name: Old Wood

attributes.content: No warning from the provincial government either, which means it's probably something with this segment of the river, rather than a Catastrophe.
attributes.name: Town Chief

attributes.content: (Salty water... looks like drinking water is the most urgent issue.)
attributes.name: Ju

attributes.content: You know this stuff?
attributes.name: Little Wood

attributes.content: Little Wood!
attributes.name: Old Wood

attributes.content: (I told you, you're the only one who can hear me.)
attributes.name: Ju

attributes.content: Drinking water is the most urgent issue. The wells in town have become polluted as well. If we can't fix the river, we'll have to try to get water from the north mountain directly.
attributes.name: Town Chief

attributes.content: But what can we do?
attributes.name: Old Man from Neighboring Town

attributes.content: ('Guide spring water into the village with bamboo.' It was one of the Carpenter's ideas, one that never came to fruition due to resource and manpower limitations at the time.
attributes.name: Ju

attributes.content: (You actually know this stuff?)
attributes.name: Little Wood

attributes.content: (Just repeat what I say. If anyone asks, tell them that you heard a traveling merchant talk about it by the riverside.)
attributes.name: Ju

attributes.content: G-Guide spring water into the village with bamboo.
attributes.name: Little Wood

attributes.content: Yes! When I was in Shangshu, I saw people make pipes out of bamboo to transport salty wellwater.
attributes.name: Returned Peddler

attributes.content: Apparently, proper setup and placement will filter out sand and prevent algae from forming, resulting in clear, clean water.
attributes.name: Returned Peddler

attributes.content: How do you know about these things?
attributes.name: Old Man from Neighboring Town

attributes.content: I heard a traveling merchant talk about it by the riverside!
attributes.name: Little Wood

attributes.content: Well, no reason not to give it a try.
attributes.name: Town Chief

attributes.content: We have a plan. Construction for spring plowing can wait until the bamboo pipes are completed.
attributes.name: Town Chief

attributes.content: It will take tremendous manpower and resources...
attributes.name: Old Wood

attributes.content: It's expensive, but it must be done. Our two downs should work together to overcome this crisis.
attributes.name: Town Chief

attributes.content: We'll follow your lead.
attributes.name: Old Man from Neighboring Town

attributes.content: Do you want to study with me now?
attributes.name: Ju

attributes.content: Not really.
attributes.name: Little Wood

attributes.content: But you seem to know a lot. 'Guide spring water into the village with bamboo'... you helped us. I guess you're not a bad rock after all.
attributes.name: Little Wood

attributes.content: But why do you want me to study with you?
attributes.name: Little Wood

attributes.content: Ahem. It's rare to meet someone who can hear me. Besides... let's just say I saw it as my duty to teach some manners into an unruly brat like you.
attributes.name: Ju

attributes.content: You talked about the mistakes you made. Can people who made mistakes become teachers?
attributes.name: Little Wood

attributes.content: All you need to know is that I can give you knowledge and help you help the others.
attributes.name: Ju

attributes.content: The storage tank outside the town is complete, and the bamboo pipes have been connected, but we haven't had time to prepare the lacquer and flax to seal the connections.
attributes.name: Old Wood

attributes.content: Spring plowing will be delayed at this rate. We need to plant seedlings more efficiently.
attributes.name: Town Chief

attributes.content: But what can we do, besides putting more resources and manpower into it?
attributes.name: Old Wood

attributes.content: There must be another way, just like these bamboo pipes.
attributes.name: Town Chief

attributes.content: Yes, this seeding tool allows seeds to be planted with the foot. It saves energy and is easier on the back.
attributes.name: Ju

attributes.content: Remember your lessons, Little Wood. What can be done to improve this contraption?
attributes.name: Ju

attributes.content: Using lighter wood for the top and a hollow design will make it lighter and more comfortable to wear, allowing it to be used for long periods!
attributes.name: Little Wood

attributes.content: Good, good.
attributes.name: Ju

attributes.content: I'll go tell the farmers!
attributes.name: Little Wood

attributes.content: A waterwheel? But it's quite different from the ones I know...
attributes.name: Ju

attributes.content: Show it to me, Little Wood!
attributes.name: Ju

attributes.content: Alright—Burdenbeast! Keep at it! I'll give you a treat when we're done here!
attributes.name: Little Wood

attributes.content: The waterwheel begins to spin, the blades picking up the water sent by the bamboo pipes and pouring it into the newly built wooden reservoir.
The splashing water form drops in the sun, then lines as it flows into the fields that have been waiting for so long.
The waterwheel brings the water into farmland, which grow the crops that sustain the people. It is a scene that Ju has seen thousands of times.
But there are still new surprises waiting in store.
You seem to know so much, but you've never seen this?
attributes.name: Little Wood

attributes.content: No. This is the first time.
attributes.name: Ju

attributes.content: We made this ourselves! The farmers figured it out as they plowed the fields year after year!
attributes.name: Little Wood

attributes.content: Using burdenbeasts to pull an axle that moves the waterwheel... it is indeed much more efficient than the old ways.
attributes.name: Ju

attributes.content: If there comes a day when all the troubles are gone, I would love to be a straw man in the fields, enjoying the peace and quiet.
attributes.name: Ju

attributes.content: You just want to slack while everyone's working in the fields!
attributes.name: Little Wood

attributes.content: Why, you insolent brat—
attributes.name: Ju

attributes.content: You have learned the theories. Now it's time to practice them.
attributes.name: Ju

attributes.content: Huh? Now?
attributes.name: Little Wood

attributes.content: A student must not be lazy. One cannot gain true knowledge through words alone, if without practice.
attributes.name: Ju

attributes.content: From this day forth, you will walk through the town with me, and try to repair any instrument that is damaged.
attributes.name: Ju

attributes.content: Do not let anyone discover the secret of the stone.
attributes.name: Ju

attributes.content: —Alright!
attributes.name: Little Wood

attributes.content: Did you see it? It's so weird!
attributes.name: Returned Peddler

attributes.content: It was nighttime. Maybe it was just a fowlbeast running across firewood in the dark.
attributes.name: Old Man from Neighboring Town

attributes.content: Not that! It was a ghastly figure the height of—the height of this little girl here!
attributes.name: Returned Peddler

attributes.content: Have you decided to go into storytelling after all your travels?
attributes.name: Town Chief

attributes.content: (See what I told you? No one must find out!)
attributes.name: Ju

attributes.content: (He said it was a 'ghastly figure'! He didn't say it was me...)
attributes.name: Little Wood

attributes.content: Speaking of strange things, I've been hearing a lot about them lately. Good things, though.
attributes.name: Old Wood

attributes.content: Ms. Wang was mourning her broken broom a few days ago. The next morning, it just fixed itself, good as new!
attributes.name: Old Wood

attributes.content: (Listen! Dad's praising me!)
attributes.name: Little Wood

attributes.content: (Wipe that grin off your face. How long did it take you to repair a single broom?)
attributes.name: Ju

attributes.content: There were other things too...
attributes.name: Old Wood

attributes.content: Maybe a deity of fixing broken things happens to be staying in town?
attributes.name: Returned Peddler

attributes.content: Deities aren't real, are they? Probably a craftsman in town just decided to do it in his free time...
attributes.name: Old Wood

attributes.content: Well, if there was one, I hope he'll pay me a visit, because I need to get the rice milling machine fixed before the autumn harvest.
attributes.name: Town Chief

attributes.content: The autumn harvest is exactly what I'm here about...
attributes.name: Old Man from Neighboring Town

attributes.content: The stalks are heavy with grain this year. It will be a good harvest.
attributes.name: Ju

attributes.content: Of course! Everyone worked so hard from spring through autumn.
attributes.name: Little Wood

attributes.content: And you're slacking off while they're working in the fields.
attributes.name: Ju

attributes.content: I'm not slacking off! How many things did you think I repaired lately?
attributes.name: Little Wood

attributes.content: All the things they broke these past few months, from shovels to brooms to buckets to carrying poles—you saw me fix them, didn't you?
attributes.name: Little Wood

attributes.content: You say that again, and I won't take you to see the new stuff in town again!
attributes.name: Little Wood

attributes.content: Why, you brat—
attributes.name: Ju

attributes.content: Can you tell me know, Mr. Ju? What was the mistake you made?
attributes.name: Little Wood

attributes.content: A long time ago, I lived with a group of people who dwelled amongst the mountains and seas. I taught them the knowledge of crafting... and they repaid me with loyalty.
attributes.name: Ju

attributes.content: But war came to their lands, in the end.
attributes.name: Ju

attributes.content: You think you should have listened to them and left that country, rather than taking them to face that king's armies—and your own kind?
attributes.name: Little Wood

attributes.content: Did you think you started the conflict between them?
attributes.name: Little Wood

attributes.content: But—I don't think leaving is the best way! Dad said that turning around to leave is the easiest thing to do... it's just running away!
attributes.name: Little Wood

attributes.content: Dad said that I'll run into a lot of tough things when I grow up, like bad harvests, or that sickness of the river a while back, and... many more things!
attributes.name: Little Wood

attributes.content: But we've got arms and legs. We can't reap without sowing!
attributes.name: Little Wood

attributes.content: That's why I don't think just going away is the best way to solve a conflict. There must be a better way!
attributes.name: Little Wood

attributes.text: The words of a young, naive child.
She has a long journey ahead of her, many things that she will need to learn about.
What solution was there, in the conflict that It instigated?
What solution is there, for all the conflicts of the land?

attributes.content: You're up early, girl.
attributes.name: Returned Peddler

attributes.content: Don't be in such a hurry, be careful—
attributes.name: Returned Peddler

attributes.content: You can't stop her. Everybody's got a smile on their faces from the bumper crop. The girl's eager to help her dad.
attributes.name: Town Chief

attributes.content: The harvest this year has certainly been remarkable...
attributes.name: Returned Peddler

attributes.content: Will you stay in town next year, or will you be seeking your fortune elsewhere again?
attributes.name: Town Chief

attributes.content: Who would want to risk the Catastrophes out there, if every year is a bumper harvest?
attributes.name: Returned Peddler

attributes.content: Yeah... say, I heard you've started writing story books, about the Stone and the strange events of our town.
attributes.name: Town Chief

attributes.content: Folks from the next town say it's really popular there. It's even selling as far as Shangshu!
attributes.name: Town Chief

attributes.content: Treat us to a nice meal for the new year if you make it big, won't you?
attributes.name: Town Chief

attributes.content: Chief...
attributes.name: Returned Peddler

attributes.content: Chief...
attributes.name: Despairing Farmer

attributes.content: We worked tirelessly from winter to autumn, building bamboo pipes, digging ditches, building canals, and working on waterwheels and other farm tools...
We poured our heart and soul into this soil, but...
There'll be a way.
attributes.name: Town Chief

attributes.content: Call on every able-bodied person in town. We need to clear up the clogged ditches first.
attributes.name: Town Chief

attributes.content: But we haven't had time to repair the tools after using them in spring and summer.
attributes.name: Old Wood

attributes.content: Then drain the water standing in the farmland first! We need to save as much rice as we can!
attributes.name: Town Chief

attributes.content: How do we do it without tools—
attributes.name: Despairing Farmer

attributes.content: Then get on repairing them! Use bowls and hands if there's no time!
attributes.name: Town Chief

attributes.content: Chief...
attributes.name: Little Wood

attributes.content: Now's not the time, girl—
attributes.name: Town Chief

attributes.content: What if... the tools are already fixed?
attributes.name: Little Wood

attributes.content: This is no time to be joking around, girl...
attributes.name: Town Chief

attributes.content: I'm not joking! Go see for yourself!
attributes.name: Little Wood

attributes.content: ...Let's go!
attributes.name: Town Chief

attributes.content: How did these tools—
attributes.name: Old Wood

attributes.content: Buckets, shovels, hoes... the tools that are broken through wear and tear now lie scattered in the old warehouse.
And most of them have been repaired—not quite good as new, but in working condition.
It's not a lot, but it's enough for now!
attributes.name: Despairing Farmer

attributes.content: Get everyone on it—
attributes.name: Town Chief

attributes.content: Slow down, careful—
attributes.name: Returned Peddler

attributes.content: Chief! What are you gonna do with the water sitting in the fields?
attributes.name: Old Man from Neighboring Town

attributes.content: The man is exhausted from a long journey. There is the smell of morning dew on him.
...You've repaired all your tools?
attributes.name: Old Man from Neighboring Town

attributes.content: Hoes, buckets, carrying poles, water wheels... with these tools, perhaps...
attributes.name: Old Man from Neighboring Town

attributes.content: We need to borrow the tools, chief!
attributes.name: Old Man from Neighboring Town

attributes.content: What are you...?
attributes.name: Old Man from Neighboring Town

attributes.content: Our town isn't in great shape either, as you can see.
attributes.name: Old Wood

attributes.content: Gan ni niang! Who was it who was talking about working together to overcome the crisis?!
attributes.name: Old Man from Neighboring Town

attributes.content: Now is not the time to fight.
attributes.name: Town Chief

attributes.content: ...I'm sorry. But even without the tools, even with bare hands, I'm sure your town can overcome the crisis if everyone works together.
attributes.name: Town Chief

attributes.content: You're really going to put this stone into a shrine?
attributes.name: Old Wood

attributes.content: You haven't let your own books get into your own head, have you? It was just a rock that the girl picked up. How could it be the 'legendary Stone' itself?
attributes.name: Old Wood

attributes.content: I thought it was only a legend at first...
attributes.name: Returned Peddler

attributes.content: But think about it... ever since it appeared, we've had broken things repair themselves, then a bumper crop, and then those tools that somehow got fixed...
attributes.name: Returned Peddler

attributes.content: Could the legends be true? I would rather err on the chance that it's real.
attributes.name: Returned Peddler

attributes.content: But we saved that harvest with our own hands, our own hard work.
attributes.name: Old Wood

attributes.content: You're not wrong, but how do you explain the tools that fixed themselves?
attributes.name: Returned Peddler

attributes.content: Maybe it was a craftsman in town...
attributes.name: Old Wood

attributes.content: How many craftsmen are there in this town?
attributes.name: Returned Peddler

attributes.content: Why did you come to the shrine? I thought you didn't believe in deities.
attributes.name: Returned Peddler

attributes.content: ...Erring on the chance that it's real, I guess.
attributes.name: Old Wood

attributes.content: What's that commotion outside?
attributes.name: Returned Peddler

attributes.content: What are you people doing—
attributes.name: Old Wood

attributes.content: Thieves! That's my stone!
attributes.name: Little Wood

attributes.content: Get out of the way, girl! We're just here for the stone!
attributes.name: Old Man from Neighboring Town

attributes.content: Being a thief is bad enough, now you're bullying a little girl?!
attributes.name: Old Wood

attributes.content: We're just borrowing it! You got lucky that the rain didn't flood your whole town, and now you won't let other people have a good harvest either?
attributes.name: Woman from Neighboring Town

attributes.content: You're rambling! Look at all the hard work we put into these farms! The bamboo pipes, tools, water wheels, rice mill... we made it with our two hands!
attributes.name: Old Wood

attributes.content: You're the one who's rambling! Why did you come to this shrine if you didn't believe in the lengendary stone?
attributes.name: Old Man from Neighboring Town

attributes.content: I—
attributes.name: Old Wood

attributes.content: The peddler's here too! You wrote the storybook, you tell us about it! Is story about this girl coming up with the seeding tool improvements real, or did you make it all up?
attributes.name: Old Man from Neighboring Town

attributes.content: ...I heard it from that farmer.
attributes.name: Returned Peddler

attributes.content: And what about the stories of this girl wandering around the town carrying the stone?
attributes.name: Old Man from Neighboring Town

attributes.content: (Glances at Little Wood)
attributes.name: Returned Peddler

attributes.content: Regardless, the stone belongs to us.
attributes.name: Returned Peddler

attributes.content: That's right!
attributes.name: Townsfolk

attributes.content: The other townsfolk yell in agreement, but the people from the neighboring town do not back down—
How did all those tools get fixed after the rain?
attributes.name: Woman from Neighboring Town

attributes.content: Why is it that your town alone weathered the crisis?
attributes.name: Woman from Neighboring Town

attributes.content: There must be some sorcery... it must be the legendary stone!
attributes.name: Woman from Neighboring Town

attributes.content: I heard everything you were talking about! Some stone? Does this look like one that makes dreams come true?
attributes.name: Little Wood

attributes.content: G-Guide spring water into the village with bamboo.
attributes.name: Little Wood

attributes.content: How do you know about these things?
attributes.name: Old Man from Neighboring Town

attributes.content: How does a little girl know about these things?!
attributes.name: Old Man from Neighboring Town

attributes.content: If it's indeed the legendary stone that brought all this, we need it, and we'll have it even if we have to fight for it—
attributes.name: Old Man from Neighboring Town

attributes.content: (Listen, girl, I have an idea to stop a fight from breaking out.)
attributes.name: Ju

attributes.content: (Mr. Ju?!)
attributes.name: Little Wood

attributes.content: (Do what you tried to do when we first met. Throw me into the river. There will be no conflict if there is no Stone.)
attributes.name: Ju

attributes.content: (You want to solve the problem by leaving?)
attributes.name: Little Wood

attributes.content: (It's the best way to go to avoid things from going out of control.)
attributes.name: Ju

attributes.content: (You have learned your lessons well... you can continue to hone your skills on your own.)
attributes.name: Ju

attributes.content: It looks like a fight might break out at any moment.
STOP IT!
attributes.name: Little Wood

attributes.content: The child's voice rings loud and clear in the tiny shrine.
(Girl—)
attributes.name: Ju

attributes.content: (Raises the stone)
attributes.name: Little Wood

attributes.content: You call this a 'legendary stone'. Look at it. Does it look like there's anything legendary about it?
attributes.name: Little Wood

attributes.content: The crowd bends in to take a closer look at the green stone lying quietly in the girl's hand. It is dull, unremarkable, looking no more magical than any ordinary stone picked up from the fields.
I-I was the one who fixed the tools after the rain!
attributes.name: Little Wood

attributes.content: You? Hah, I guess the stories have gotten to your head, girl!
attributes.name: Old Man from Neighboring Town

attributes.content: ...Watch me!
attributes.name: Little Wood

attributes.content: The girl takes a broken hoe and ties the broken pieces together with vines. The hoe is now even stronger than it used to be.
Haven't we been planting our rice with our own hands all these years?
attributes.name: Little Wood

attributes.content: Why do we stop trusting our hands after one rain? Why do we have to believe in some stone... believe in deities?
attributes.name: Little Wood

attributes.content: Why do we wish for a stone to solve all our problems?
attributes.name: Little Wood

attributes.content: Why are we fighting like this over a rock?
attributes.name: Little Wood

attributes.content: We should be counting on ourselves!
attributes.name: Little Wood

attributes.content: The sun is pale today. The empty fields lie fallow, like a heavy snow had just fallen.
There!
attributes.name: Little Wood

attributes.content: You really let the girl build a strawman around the stone and put it in the field.
attributes.name: Old Man from Neighboring Town

attributes.content: This is both a... closure for what happened that day, and a symbol of hope that next year will be a good harvest.
attributes.name: Town Chief

attributes.content: If there's really a deity in the stone... I think he'll be glad to see us work together and make all this land fields of golden grain, extending as far as the river banks.
attributes.name: Town Chief

attributes.content: For the first time in centuries, Ju has a body again.
If there comes a day when all the troubles are gone, I would love to be a straw man in the fields, enjoying the peace and quiet.
attributes.name: Little Wood

attributes.content: Seriously, where did the girl learn all these words?
attributes.name: Town Chief
